title: Thirumayam Fort
tagline: Thirumayam Fort — heritage city of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: The Thirumayam Fort is a 40-acre wide fortress in the town of Thirumayam in Pudukkottai-Karaikudi Highway in Pudukkottai District, Tamil Nadu, India.
overview.description: The Thirumayam Fort is a 40-acre wide fortress in the town of Thirumayam in Pudukkottai-Karaikudi Highway in Pudukkottai District, Tamil Nadu, India. It was constructed by Vijaya Raghunatha Sethupathi, the Raja of Ramnad in 1687. Later the fort was handed over to his brother in law Raghunatha Raya Tondaiman. The fort has been extensively renovated by Archaeological Survey of India in 2012.

topPlaces.name: Thirumayam
topPlaces.description: Thirumayam is a town located in Pudukkottai district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is a place of historical importance located about 22 km from the capital city of Pudukkottai as well as from the city of Karaikudi.

topPlaces.name: Sathyagirisvarar Temple
topPlaces.description: Sathyagirisvarar Temple is a Shiva temple located in Thirumayam, Pudukottai district, Tamil Nadu, India.

topPlaces.name: Sathyamurthi Perumal Temple, Thirumayam
topPlaces.description: Sathyamurthi Perumal Temple in Thirumayam, a panchayat town in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu, is dedicated to the Hindu god Vishnu. Constructed in the Dravidian style of architecture, the temple is glorified in the Nalayira Divya Prabandham, the early medieval Tamil canon of the Alvar saints from the 6th–9th centuries CE. It is one of the 108 Divya Desams dedicated to Vishnu, who is worshipped as Sathyamurthi Perumal and his consort Lakshmi as Ujeevana Thayar.

topPlaces.name: Rayavaram, Tamil Nadu
topPlaces.description: Rayavaram is a village Panchayat in Pudukkottai district in Tamil Nadu, India.

topPlaces.name: Thekkattur
topPlaces.description: Thekkattur is a village in the Arimalam revenue block of Pudukkottai district, Tamil Nadu, India.

topPlaces.name: Kattubava Mosque
topPlaces.description: The Kattubava Mosque, also known as the Kattubava Pallivasal, is a Sufi mosque located in the village of Kathvapallivasal, in the Pudukkottai district of the state of Tamil Nadu, India. The mosque is situated 30 kilometres (19 mi) from Pudukkottai on the Thirumayam-Madurai highway.

topPlaces.name: Nachandupatti
topPlaces.description: Nachandupatti is a small town located 16 kilometres from Pudukkottai in the Pudukkottai district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. Many members of the Nagarathar groups are among its inhabitants.

topPlaces.name: Kothamangalam, Tamil Nadu
topPlaces.description: Kothamangalam is a panchayat town in the Sivaganga district, in the state of Tamil Nadu, India. Kothamangalam Pin code is 630105 and postal head office is Kothamangalam (Sivaganga).

items.note: Thirumayam is a town located in Pudukkottai district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is a place of historical importance located about 22 km from the capital city of Pudukkottai as well as from the city of Karaikudi.
Sathyagirisvarar Temple is a Shiva temple located in Thirumayam, Pudukottai district, Tamil Nadu, India.

Sathyamurthi Perumal Temple in Thirumayam, a panchayat town in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu, is dedicated to the Hindu god Vishnu. Constructed in the Dravidian style of architecture, the temple is glorified in the Nalayira Divya Prabandham, the early medieval Tamil canon of the Alvar saints from the 6th–9th centuries CE. It is one of the 108 Divya Desams dedicated to Vishnu, who is worshipped as Sathyamurthi Perumal and his consort Lakshmi as Ujeevana Thayar.
Rayavaram is a village Panchayat in Pudukkottai district in Tamil Nadu, India.
Thekkattur is a village in the Arimalam revenue block of Pudukkottai district, Tamil Nadu, India.

The Kattubava Mosque, also known as the Kattubava Pallivasal, is a Sufi mosque located in the village of Kathvapallivasal, in the Pudukkottai district of the state of Tamil Nadu, India. The mosque is situated 30 kilometres (19 mi) from Pudukkottai on the Thirumayam-Madurai highway.
Nachandupatti is a small town located 16 kilometres from Pudukkottai in the Pudukkottai district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. Many members of the Nagarathar groups are among its inhabitants.
Kothamangalam is a panchayat town in the Sivaganga district, in the state of Tamil Nadu, India. Kothamangalam Pin code is 630105 and postal head office is Kothamangalam (Sivaganga).

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Thirumayam Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Thirumayam Fort is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Thirumayam Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Tiruchirappalli International Airport (58 km) and the nearest railway station is Tiruchirappalli Junction (62 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Thirumayam Fort?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Thirumayam Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Thirumayam Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Thirumayam Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Thirumayam Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Thirumayam, Sathyagirisvarar Temple, Sathyamurthi Perumal Temple, Thirumayam, Rayavaram, Tamil Nadu, Thekkattur.
